name: Web App
purpose: Next.js App Router application with the main lobby, localized rules dialog, disabled multiplayer entry, settings dialog, local stats, shared audio controls, locale switching, and the single-player Domino Poker game UI.
risks: AppShell owns lobby/game state, locale, audio settings, and game-session lifecycle.
DominoPokerGame has timer-driven AI turns and delayed trick completion; UI changes can affect turn flow.
The multiplayer button should remain disabled in the main lobby unless the product direction changes.

name: Local Stats
purpose: Browser-only local statistics and game-session tracking for the lobby, persisted in localStorage.
risks: Functions are intended for client-side use; guard any future server usage carefully.
Session start, finish, and abandon paths should stay best-effort so local storage failures do not block starting a single-player game.
Do not add external stats APIs without updating the local-only architecture decision.

name: Core Rules
purpose: Pure TypeScript Domino Poker rules, tile handling, bidding, legal-play validation, scoring, round state, game state, and CPU AI decisions.
risks: Rule changes can alter scoring, legal-play validation, dealer rotation, and AI decisions.
Docs contain some stale scoring details; prefer current tests and implementation when they conflict.
The package emits ESM files through a postbuild import-fix script.

name: Documentation
purpose: Public README, license, screenshots, and human-readable rules, scoring examples, and strategy notes for Domino Poker.
risks: Some documentation may lag behind tested code behavior.
Use docs as reference material, not as proof that implementation is current.
